title: Surface sealing for implants
abstract: The present invention relates to an implant (1) for insertion in a body lumen, wherein at least a portion of the surface (10) is arranged to contact a wall of the body lumen and/or bodily fluid flowing through the lumen when the implant (1) is inserted in the body lumen. The abovementioned portion of surface (10) is covered with a surface sealing (2) which is designed to dissolve within about 30 seconds when inserting the implant (1) in the body lumen, such that this portion of surface (10) is exposed to the body lumen. The present invention also relates to a method of manufacturing an implant (1) as above described, comprising the steps of obtaining an implant (1) with a surface (10); providing at least a portion of such surface (10) with target characteristics; and covering such at least a portion of surface (10) with a surface sealing (2) to preserve the target characteristics. The present invention also refers to an implant set (100) comprising an implant (1) as defined and to a use of such an implant (1) for treating an animal or a human body, wherein the treatment procedure comprises dissolving the surface sealing (2) covering at least a portion of a surface (10) of the implant (1) by flushing the surface (10) with a dissolving solution.
claims: Claims CLAI MS 1 . Implant (1 ; 19) for insertion in a body lumen, the implant comprising a surface (10; 109), at least a portion of said surface (10; 109) being arranged to contact a wall of said body lumen and/or bodily fluid flowing therethrough when said implant (1 ; 19) is inserted in said body lumen, characterized in that said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) is covered with a surface sealing (2; 29) which is soluble when inserting the implant (1 ; 19) in said body lumen, such that said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) is exposed to said body lumen. 2. The implant (1 ; 19) of claim 1 , wherein said surface sealing (2; 29) is designed to dissolve within 30 seconds 3. The implant (1 ; 19) of claim 1 or 2, wherein said surface sealing (2; 29) comprises, or consists of, a soluble carbohydrate, a soluble polymer, a soluble ionic compound or a combination thereof. 4. The implant (1 ; 19) of claim 3, wherein said soluble carbohydrate is a monosaccharide or a sugar alcohol, such as Threitol, Erythritol, Glucose, Fructose, Sorbitol, Galactose, Galactitol, Mannose, Mannitol, Xylitol, Myo-inositol or similar, organic acids such as citric acid, or other substances such as vitamin C. 5. The implant (1 ; 19) of claim 3, wherein said soluble carbohydrate is a di- or a trisaccharide, such as Trehalose, Maltotriose, Lactose, Lactulose, Palatinose, Sucrose or similar. 6. The implant (1 ; 19) of claim 3, wherein said surface sealing (2; 29) comprises a composition made of monosaccharides and/or disaccharides and/or trisaccharides and/or polymeric sugars. 7. The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) is a plain surface which preferably lacks a substantial roughness or waviness of its topology or any substantial texture or a coating or a combination thereof. 8. The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) is made of a metal or of a metal alloy, or of a polymer, or of a ceramic material, or of a combination thereof. 9. The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, being a vascular stent; or a flow diverter; or an ocular stent; or a coil or web-like structure for the treatment of vascular aneurysm; or a heart valve; or a cage of a heart valve; or a part of a cardiac pacemaker such as an electrode; or a shunt. 10. The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) is provided with target characteristics, preferably hydrophilicity. 1 1 . The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the surface sealing (2; 29) is homogeneous. 12. The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the surface sealing (2; 29) is seamlessly covering said at least a portion of surface (10; 109). 13. The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the surface sealing (2; 29) is gas-tight. 14. The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the surface sealing (2; 29) is configured to be dissolved when being inserted to a target location in the body lumen. 15. The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the surface sealing (2; 29) is configured to be dissolved when being implanted at the target location. 16. The implant (1 ; 19) of any one of the preceding claims, wherein the surface sealing (2; 29) is configured to be dissolved when arriving the target location. 17. Method of manufacturing an implant (1 ; 19) for insertion in a body lumen, comprising: obtaining an implant (1 ; 19) with a surface (10; 109); providing at least a portion of said surface (10; 109), which is arranged to contact a wall of said body lumen and/or bodily fluid flowing therethrough, with target characteristics; covering said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) with a surface sealing (2; 29) to preserve said target characteristics, wherein said surface sealing (2; 29) is soluble when inserting the implant (1 ; 19) in said body lumen. 18. The manufacturing method of claim 17, wherein covering said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) with a surface sealing (2; 29) further comprises: providing a solution of a sealing agent; immersing said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) in said solution, spraying said solution on said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) or rinsing said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) with said solution; and drying said sealing agent on said at least a portion of surface to form said surface sealing (2; 29). 19. The manufacturing method of claim 17 or 18, comprising a step of arranging said implant (1 ; 19) in or on an insertion device (3). 20. The manufacturing method of claim 19, comprising flushing the delivery device (3) together with the implant (1 ; 19) with a solution of a sealing agent. 21 . The manufacturing method of any one of claims 17 to 20, comprising a step of sterilising said implant (1 ; 19) by applying a radiation or a gas, subsequently to said step of covering said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) with the surface sealing (2; 29). 22. The manufacturing method of any one of claims 17 to 21 , wherein said surface sealing comprises, or consists of, at least a soluble or particularly water-soluble carbohydrate. 23. The manufacturing method of claim 22, wherein said soluble carbohydrate is a monosaccharide or a sugar alcohol, such as Threitol, Erythritol, Glucose, Fructose, Sorbitol, Galactose, Galactitol, Mannose, Mannitol, Xylitol, Myo-inositol or similar, or a di- or a trisaccharide, such as Trehalose, Maltotriose, Lactose, Lactulose, Palatinose, Sucrose or similar, organic acids such as citric acid, or other substances such as vitamin C. 24. The manufacturing method of any one of claims 17 to 23, wherein said target characteristics comprise hydrophilicity target characteristics. 25. The manufacturing method of any one of claims 17 to 24, wherein covering said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) with a surface sealing (2; 29) comprises configuring the surface sealing to be dissolved when being inserted to a target location in the body lumen. 26. The manufacturing method of any one of claims 17 to 25, wherein the surface sealing is configured to be dissolved when being implanted at the target location. 27. Use of an implant (1 ; 19) according to any one of claims 1 to 16 for treating an animal or a human body, comprising at least partially dissolving a surface sealing (2; 29) covering at least a portion of a surface (10; 109) of the implant (1 ; 19) by flushing said surface (10; 109) with a dissolving solution. 28. The use of claim 27, comprising exposing said at least a portion of surface (10; 109) to said body lumen, when said surface sealing (2; 29) is dissolved. 29. The use of claim 27 or 28, wherein the dissolving solution is deliverable to the implant (1 ; 19) via an insertion device (3) in fluid communication therewith. 30. Implant set (100) comprising an implant (1 ; 19) according to any one of claims 1 to 16 and a package (4) configured to protect the surface (10; 109) of said implant (1 ; 19). 31 . The implant set (100) of claim 30, wherein said package (4) is configured to store said implant (1 ; 19) in a dry condition. 32. The implant set (100) of claim 30 or 31 , comprising a pouch (5), configured to receive said implant (1 ; 19) and said package (4) for storage, said pouch (5) being permeable to a gaseous sterilising agent. 33. The Implant set (100) of any one of claims 30 to 32, comprising a pouch (5), configured to receive said implant (1 ; 19) and said package (4) for storage, said pouch (5) being radiation sterilisable. 34. The Implant set (100) of any one of claims 30 to 33, further comprising an insertion device (3) for inserting said implant (1 ; 19) in a body lumen, wherein said implant (1 ; 19) is arrangeable in or on said insertion device (3). 35. The Implant set (100) of claim 34, wherein the insertion device (3) for inserting said implant (1 ; 19) in a body lumen is a catheter (3). 36. The Implant set (100) of any one of claims 30 to 35, wherein the package (4) comprises a cover for opening and/or closing the package (4). 37. The Implant set (100) of claim 36, wherein the cover is equipped with a pierceable structure such as a septum or the like. 38. The Implant set (100) of any one of claims 30 to 37, wherein the implant (1 ; 19) is flushable directly within the package (4) by, e.g., a penetrating needle, without the need of prior removal of said implant (1 ; 19) from the package (4). 39. The Implant set (100) of any one of claims 30 to 38, wherein the implant (1 ; 19) is solvably bound to the package (4).
